items.title: Family's 'worst nightmare' after purchasing home filled with defects

children.content: A young family invested their life savings into their dream home only to discover it was filled with defects, as the state's consumer watchdog issues a public warning notice about the builder responsible and seven other traders.

image.altText: A woman and a man holding a young child in front of a house

items.title: SA farmers celebrate dramatic turnaround after record rain

children.content: Twelve months after drought left paddocks bare, record rainfall in parts of South Australia has left some farmers smiling at significant improvements.

image.altText: A man kneels amongst a green crop on a farm.

items.title: SA government says crackdown is leading to illicit tobacco price increase

children.content: South Australia's Consumer and Business Affairs Minister says a price increase for illegal tobacco noticed by inspectors within stores shows the trade is being disrupted.

image.altText: Cigarettes and vapes on a table

items.title: Highland cows encounters bring 'positive impact', agritourism operators say

children.content: Traipsing around a farm in the wake of a highland cow or donkey might seem like a quaint rural pastime, but those in the business of agritourism believe there are other benefits.

image.altText: Two large shaggy cows in a green paddock. One is looking directly atthe camera, the other is grazing the grass.

items.title: Homelessness service concerned by changing demographic in clients

children.content: As cost of living and housing pressures intensify, a South Australian homelessness service says more young women and single mothers are reaching out for support.

image.altText: A person sleeping wrapped in a blanket in the middle of a grassy reserve, city streets and cars in the background

items.title: Local mayor 'shocked' at man's death after 'ineffective' taser arrest

children.content: A local mayor has expressed shock at the death of a man during an arrest at Clare, in South Australia's Mid North on Sunday, as the state's police minister says he is confident the incident will be thoroughly investigated.

gallery.altText: A man in a checked shirt and sunglasses stands in front of a row of green trees

thumbnail.altText: A man in a checked shirt and sunglasses stands in front of a row of green trees

items.title: 30 years of Pokémon: The 'immersive' phenomenon still finding new fans

children.content: Three decades after the franchise that coined the phrase "Gotta catch 'em all!" was first released, parents are sharing their love of the franchise with their children.

image.altText: A man in a pokemon hat with his son with pokemon toys and photos

items.title: With his pet chihuahua and trusty gopher, 97yo grazier won't slow down

children.content: At 97, Doug Harrison still scoots around his outback station, doing as much work as he can. As he nears a century of living, he reflects on memorable moments over the decades, with a cuppa in hand and his trusty chihuahua by his side.

image.altText: a man with black sunglasses holding the steering wheel of a vehicle with a white small dog sitting next to him

items.title: Lorna has lived through two world wars, two pandemics and 26 Australian prime ministers

children.content: After a couple of years as the country's oldest woman, Lorna Henstridge is still going strong.

image.altText: An elderly woman holding an oval framed photo of a young child wearing Elizabethan costume

items.title: 'Very troubled project': Hospital cost blowout concerns treasurer

children.content: The new Women's and Children's Hospital was priced at $3.2 billion in 2022, before inflation and other pressures sent costs in the construction industry soaring.

image.altText: A man in a navy suit sits on a chair inside looking at the camera with a neutral expression.

items.title: Mother creates modest activewear to empower herself and others

children.content: Yasmin Jammoul was sick of wearing oversized men's T-shirts to the gym when she started exercising postpartum, so she created her own clothing line.

image.altText: A woman with a dark green hijab and black outfit sitting on a chair smiling.

items.title: PM pays tribute to 'personal friend' and former neighbour Richard Scolyer

children.content: Loved ones of former Australian of the Year Richard Scolyer have paid tribute to his legacy, with his family saying he "wanted cancer patients to know they weren't alone".

articles.title: What is glioblastoma and why is it so hard to treat?

children.content: Glioblastoma claims the lives of 200,000 people each year globally. Despite decades of research and funding, survival rates remain low.

image.altText: An x-ray type image showing a tumour inside a human brain.

articles.title: Richard Scolyer, who challenged the world to 'be brave, be bold', dies aged 59

children.content: The 2024 Australian of the Year Richard Scolyer has died. In his final message he urged all Australians to "pursue their dreams and passion with humility, love and compassion".

image.altText: Tight close-up poortrait of a man with light hair and eyebrows, smiling, wearing a blue-collared shirt. Greenery blurred in back

items.title: Mihocek experienced 'lack of feeling' before hospital visit says coach

children.content: Melbourne claims an eight-point win over Collingwood at the MCG as Brody Mihocek is taken to hospital after a serious injury.

image.altText: Billy Frampton calls for a stretcher for Brody Mihocek

items.title: Sentencing of officer Benedict Bryant a rare moment of accountability

children.content: Inside a quiet district courtroom in the heart of Sydney’s bustling CBD, a historic moment has unfolded involving a NSW Police sergeant.

image.altText: Lachlan is standing beside some stairs holding a photo of Jai in a brown photoframe. He is wearing a white t-shirt

items.title: Undersea volcano inundates island with 'desert' of stone

children.content: Residents are concerned about access to food and fresh water as pumice stone makes travel difficult for Papua New Guinea's seafaring communities.

gallery.altText: Two people with sticks walk along a coastline with stone floating on the ocean behind them
Brown rocks obscure the ocean which laps at a shoreline lined with green trees
A cloud of white smoke steams up from under a deep blue ocean

items.title: Australian dollar dives to two-month low as interest rate fears grip markets

children.content: The Australian dollar falls to its lowest level in over two months, just over 70 cents to the US dollar, amid a renewed surge in the greenback as global interest rates, including US interest rates, rise.

image.altText: An Australian dollar coin placed on a US bank note.

items.title: Celebrities slip and slide in first Big Freeze event since Neale Daniher's death

children.content: The MCG has hosted the first Big Freeze MND fundraiser since the death of AFL legend and motor neurone disease campaigner, Neale Daniher.

image.altText: Weather presenter Sam Mac participates in the Big Freeze event dressed as children's character Bluey

items.title: Top neurologists say regulator ignored warnings as weed killer decision looms

children.content: Australia's leading movement disorder specialists say the chemical regulator has not engaged with their concerns about paraquat, despite growing evidence linking the herbicide to Parkinson's disease.

gallery.altText: A man wearing dark jumper and beige puffer coat speaks, seen from behind the shoulder of someone wearing purple

thumbnail.altText: A man wearing dark jumper and beige puffer coat speaks, seen from behind the shoulder of someone wearing purple

items.title: Rider killed in Finke Desert Race crash identified as Matthew Bielefeld

children.content: A motorcyclist who died while competing in this weekend's Finke Desert Race outside Alice Springs has been identified as Matthew Bielefeld.

image.altText: Two photos of a brunette man smiling, he is alongside a motorbike in one image and standing with a blonde woman in the other.

items.title: Secrets of Indigenous astronomy revealed in new exhibition

children.content: Newcastle Art Gallery presents the distinctive and intricate work of the Torres Strait Islander artist in its first major solo survey following a four-year refurbishment.

image.altText: A Torres Strait Islander man with a grey beard standing with his arms crossed in front of a large artwork.

items.title: Lentils can cost just a few dollars. Here are five tasty ways to use them

children.content: From soups to bolognese and oven bakes, lentils are a versatile and affordable meal option.

gallery.altText: A speckled white bowl of lentil bolognese ragu topped with vegan parmesan cheese and fresh basil.

thumbnail.altText: A speckled white bowl of lentil bolognese ragu topped with vegan parmesan cheese and fresh basil.

items.title: Tasmania's population boom has gone bust and it spells trouble

children.content: From being a hot destination 10 years ago, Tasmania's population growth has ground to a halt. Big changes will be needed to reverse the trend, experts say.

image.altText: Ellie Warmbrunn smiles at the camera.

items.title: Indigenous families want 'crisis' investigated after 15 deaths in six months

children.content: Remote Indigenous communities across Australia are painfully aware that their life expectancy rates are worsening — and in one Northern Territory community, residents are calling for an investigation into a local death rate they feel is "not normal".

image.altText: Aboriginal senior woman and younger Aboriginal man sitting together, serious expression, in front of green leaves.

items.title: The electric classic car that now drives like a 'pocket rocket'

children.content: It may look like a small vintage car, but an EV conversion has brought it "up to speed" to its modern counterparts.

Blue small car on the road

thumbnail.altText: David Dyer kneeling next to blue small classic car

items.title: When a detective told a witness Joanna was pregnant, she took police to VCAT

children.content: A woman who says she suffered life-changing stress and anxiety — and later, the death of one of her babies — after a Victoria Police officer unlawfully disclosed that she was pregnant wants her story known.

image.altText: A woman with brunette hair poses for a photo in a leafy setting wearing a pink top and blue scarf

items.title: Anthropic has its eyes on Australia and a large slice of its electricity output

children.content: The world's biggest tech titans see Australia as a promising investment opportunity for their AI ambitions, but some fear the federal government's regulatory retreat could lead to harm.

image.altText: A person holding an iPhone with Character.aI, ChatGPT, Claude and Grok chatbot apps on screen.

items.title: The 20-year-old Australian named one of the hottest players ahead of the FIFA World Cup

children.content: Nestory Irankunda is set for a breakout moment at the FIFA World Cup as the face of a changing Socceroos team featuring six players of African descent.

gallery.altText: Nestory Irankunda takes a selfie with fans after a Socceroos game.

thumbnail.altText: Nestory Irankunda takes a selfie with fans after a Socceroos game.

items.title: Kings and Queens of the Desert crowned as outback Finke race ends

children.content: Returning legends of the Finke Desert Race, David Walsh and Travis Robinson, have been crowned Kings of the Desert at the gruelling two-day off-road race, with Walsh winning on bikes for a record-equalling sixth time and Robinson claiming victory on cars.

image.altText: A small group of people in car racing outfits standing together on a stage and smiling, with one holding a winner's shield high.

items.title: Korda wins US Women's Open title with perilous final putt

children.content: Nelly Korda claims the US Women's Open for her second consecutive major victory, holding off Charley Hull and Gaby Lopez by one shot in Los Angeles.

image.altText: Nelly Korda holds the US Women's Open trophy.

items.title: Arnhem Land culture takes centre stage at Barunga Festival 2026

children.content: Sammy Wurrawilya has taken out the title of spear-throwing champion at this year's Barunga Festival with an impressive effort of more than 30 metres.

avatar.alt: A journalist wears a button-up shirt and serious facial expression.

items.title: Trump storms out of interview after grilling on 'crooked' election claims

children.content: US President Donald Trump angrily storms out of an interview with American broadcaster NBC after he was questioned about his unfounded claims of election fraud.

image.altText: A man in a navy suit and red tie, sitting for an interview

items.title: UK, France and Germany back Zelenskyy's call for meeting with Putin

children.content: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy has received the backing of the leaders of the UK, France and Germany in calling for direct ceasefire talks with Russia.

image.altText: Britain's Prime Minister Keir Starmer, France's President Emmanuel Macron, Ukrain'e President Volodymyr Zelenskyy pose for photo

items.title: Calls for kindness as community mourns death of shark attack victim

children.content: The Albany community is still coming to terms with the loss of resident Daniel Turpin after he did not survive a shark attack at the weekend.

image.altText: Compositie image of a man on left and marina on right.

items.title: Perth actor wants police to pay after apartment 'destroyed' during murder probe

children.content: Troy Coward's investment property was the scene of a brutal murder involving his tenants in 2012. More than 13 years later, he is fighting for compensation from police over the impact he says their investigation had on his health and finances.

image.altText: Man with short hair and dark brown blazer, arms folded, with trees and pathway in background

items.title: Rare bird sighting excites twitchers

children.content: Birdwatchers from across Perth and even interstate have flocked to WA's Midwest after the sighting of a black-headed gull.

gallery.altText: a silver grey gull with black head wings spread in flight over a beach
